title: Mekanism: QIO Autocrafter
description: Adds a Mekanism-integrated autocrafting machine that uses the QIO system as its only inventory, allowing fully automated crafting directly from your stored resources.
body:
## WORK-IN PROGRESS
# QIO AutoCrafter

**Autocrafting for Mekanism's QIO system — no cables, no interfaces, no pattern chests.**

QIO AutoCrafter adds a single machine that plans and executes crafting recipes directly
against a QIO frequency. Ask it for an item, and it figures out the whole recipe tree on
its own: which intermediates to craft, how many of each, and whether your stored materials
are enough — then crafts everything virtually, straight from and into your QIO storage.

---

##  Features

- **Recursive auto-crafting** — request a deep Mekanism item (induction cells, control
  circuits, energy cubes...) and the machine resolves the full recipe chain automatically.
  No patterns to encode, ever.
- **QIO-native** — materials are taken from the bound QIO frequency and results go back
  into it. The machine itself stores nothing but energy.
- **Smart planning** — runs asynchronously off the server thread, prefers materials you
  already have, understands tag variants (any log → planks), and reports *exactly* which
  base materials you're missing and how many.
- **Missing Materials panel** — live side panel listing every missing item with its icon,
  the amount needed and how much is already available, color-coded red/orange/green as you
  restock.
- **Job queue** — queue up to 24 requests; completed jobs clean themselves up, failed ones
  tell you why, and cancelling returns every reserved material to the QIO.
- **Safe by design** — materials are reserved before crafting and rolled back on any
  failure. Chunk unloads and server restarts mid-craft don't dupe or void items.
- **Multiplayer ready** — respects Mekanism frequency security (Public / Trusted /
  Private): nobody can drain a private frequency through your machine.
- **Upgrades** — supports Speed and Energy upgrades.

---

##  Interface

![UI](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/0db2de70dc7325ec813afd37df8e27e0e25b60c5.png)

Search any craftable item, set the amount (up to 9,999,999), press **Start** and watch the
queue. The activity bar shows what the machine is doing at all times.

![UIMissing](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/7e033bc1301e7b1d9e4db36395712ec7f953a339_0.webp)

The **Missing** button opens the side panel: every missing material with live availability
from your QIO, updating as you restock.

---

## 🔧 Getting started

1. **Craft the machine** (endgame recipe — see below).
2. **Bind a frequency**: right-click the machine with a *Portable QIO Dashboard* that has
   your frequency selected.
3. **Power it** (any Forge Energy / Mekanism cable works).
4. Right-click to open the GUI, search an item, set the amount and press **Start**.

---

##  Crafting recipe

![Crafting](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b95d79c6c0a7d296a16d1fb8681563fa4609a215.png)

| | | |
|---|---|---|
| Ultimate Induction Provider | Supermassive QIO Drive | Ultimate Induction Provider |
| Supermassive QIO Drive | **Robit** | Supermassive QIO Drive |
| Ultimate Induction Provider | Supermassive QIO Drive | Ultimate Induction Provider |

It's meant to be a late-game reward — by the time you can afford four Supermassive QIO
Drives, you *really* don't want to hand-craft induction matrices anymore.

---

##  Requirements

| | |
|---|---|
| Minecraft | **1.20.1** (Forge 47.4+) |
| Required | **Mekanism 10.4.16+** |
| Sides | Client **and** server (same version on both) |

Works great alongside Mekanism Generators / Tools / Extras.

---

## ⚠️ Good to know ⚠️

- If a recipe tree is too deep for the planning budget, the job fails with a clear message;
  queue a simpler intermediate first and retry.
- Frequency binding currently requires the Portable QIO Dashboard (no in-GUI frequency
  selector yet).
